title: status-pipe repo configuration
description: Committed at .status-pipe/config.json: this repo's conventions — epic dir, inventory label, ticketing source, trust mode, attribution. Read by the plugin (binding) and the extension (display hints). Trust and execution config is read from the primary checkout's working tree only (anchored at the git common dir) — never from the branch a linked worktree has checked out, and never from a fetched ref.

dir.description: Epic folder name relative to the repo root

label.description: Forge label / JQL label selecting tickets for the agent's inventory

assignees.description: Optional routing filter, orthogonal to trust: when present, inventory keeps only tickets assigned to one of these identities (intersected with any trust-mode assignee filter). Absent ⇒ no assignee scoping. Routing only — being listed here never grants authority to drive the agent (that is trust.operators).

oneOf.description: Split form for Bitbucket+Jira repos: per-channel stable ids (Bitbucket account UUID, Jira account id), never display names

staleWorkerMinutes.description: Committed source of truth for the worker-heartbeat staleness threshold; the orchestrator echoes it into orchestrator.json

review.description: Tuning for the worker's adversarial review loop (the 'hardening' phase). Absent = defaults below; the loop is on by default.

enabled.description: When false, the worker skips the hardening loop and goes straight from implement to submit (the old single self-review still applies).

blockSeverity.description: Lowest defect severity that blocks convergence. 'medium' blocks high+medium (default); 'high' loosens the gate to correctness/security only.

cleanWavesRequired.description: Consecutive clean waves (no blocking defects) needed before the loop converges and the worker submits.

capWaves.description: Maximum waves before the loop escalates to the operator without converging.

trust.description: Who may drive the agent through forge comments. The plugin refuses to tick a public repo without a declared mode; a private repo without this block defaults to single-maintainer with the authenticated forge user.

oneOf.description: Split form for Bitbucket+Jira repos: per-channel stable ids (Bitbucket account UUID, Jira account id), never display names

minAssociation.description: Optional additional GitHub author_association requirement (e.g. OWNER, MEMBER). Never sufficient alone.

commentPrefix.description: Prepended to every agent-posted comment. Social transparency only — never a trust input.

prBanner.description: Placed near the top of every agent-authored PR description, naming the responsible operator

includeAgentId.description: Extend the prefix with the posting context (epic/part), for repos where several epics' workers share an account
